text: How has the final energy consumption per capita in Switzerland changed from 2000 to today?
answers: It has decreased by more than 15% thanks to efficiency

text: In the United States, which energy source historically surpassed coal for the first time in 2022 in electricity generation?
What percentage of Switzerland's domestic electricity generation is covered by nuclear power plants?
Which US state is the absolute leader in installed solar energy capacity?
In Switzerland, nearly 100% of which of these energy sources must be imported from abroad?
Which economic sector consumes the largest share of total primary energy in the United States?
What is currently the most frequently installed heating system in new homes in Switzerland to reduce the carbon footprint?
The United States has become one of the world's largest exporters of which energy resource thanks to unconventional extraction techniques (fracking)?
What is Swiss hydroelectric production mainly composed of?
Approximately how many commercial nuclear reactors are active in the United States, making it the world's largest producer of nuclear energy?
The Swiss electricity bill includes a 'grid surcharge' (Netzzuschlag). What is its main purpose?
answers: To pay fines for CO2 emissions
To finance the development of renewable energies and efficiency
To import oil from foreign countries
To keep old gas plants running

text: In which region of the United States is the vast majority of the country's hydroelectric power production concentrated?
Which international event pushed the Swiss government to decide in 2011 to gradually phase out nuclear energy?
answers: The signing of the Paris Agreement

text: How many main power grids (Interconnections) make up the contiguous United States grid?
answers: Three main grids (Eastern, Western, and Texas)
Fifty grids, one for each state, with no exchanges

text: In both Switzerland and America, the energy transition focuses heavily on the 'electrification' of which sector historically dominated by oil?
How much energy is saved by recycling an aluminum can compared to producing a new one?
Why is it important to recycle paper?
answers: Because it helps save trees and energy

text: What is currently the most widely used renewable energy source globally for electricity generation?
Which nation is currently the world's largest producer and consumer of solar and wind energy?
Which European country is famous for generating almost 100% of its electricity using a mix of geothermal and hydroelectric energy?
In the context of global energy consumption, in which of these sectors do renewable energies struggle the most to establish themselves rapidly?
What exactly is meant by 'biomass' when discussing renewable energy consumption?
answers: Plant or animal organic material burned or processed to produce energy

text: What does the concept of 'intermittency' represent, which often limits the exclusive use of certain renewable energies on the grid?
answers: The fact that solar panels break often
The physiological voltage drop in old power grids
The inability to produce energy when there is no sun or wind
The need to turn off windmills at night

text: Which continent, thanks to the massive use of hydroelectricity (especially in nations like Brazil), has the highest percentage of electricity produced from renewable sources?
Globally, modern renewable energies (excluding the traditional burning of wood for cooking) cover approximately what percentage of total primary energy consumption?
What does 'grid parity' mean in the renewable energy market?
answers: When an electrical grid covers the entire globe
When renewable energy costs the same or less than electricity produced from traditional fossil sources
When governments legally ban the use of coal
When solar and wind energy produce exactly the same amount of electricity

text: Which supporting technology is considered essential to massively increase the consumption of solar and wind energy in the coming decades?
answers: High-capacity storage systems (e.g., large battery networks)
New types of ultra-filtered coal plants
The use of pure gold cables to reduce heat loss
The construction of space nuclear reactors
